signals.headline: Fed Holds Rates Steady Amid Surging Q2 GDP Revision and Tariff Uncertainty; Powell Vows to Remain Chair Despite DOJ Prob
signals.raw:
Fed Holds Rates Steady Amid Surging Q2 GDP Revision and Tariff Uncertainty; Powell Vows to Remain Chair Despite DOJ Probe | NO | HIGH confidence**

Jerome Powell confirmed on April 16 that Q1 GDP growth has slowed due to modest consumer spending and strong imports ahead of tariffs, with initial data due soon; inflation eased to 2.3% total PCE and 2.6% core but remains above 2% target.[1] Commerce
matched_markets: Will Powell say "Tariff inflation" during April press conference?
